data.title: Lululemon founder criticizes company's board over see-through leggings, stock price falls.
data.description: Chip Wilson, the founder of Lululemon Athletica, Inc. (NASDAQ: LULU), has criticized the company's board of directors for recent quality control issues, labeling them as a "new low" for the yoga-wear giant. Wilson, the largest individual shareholder despite stepping down years ago, slammed the board's management in a viral LinkedIn post. He specifically highlighted see-through leggings and declining product standards as the consequence of the board's actions. LULU stock is currently experiencing a decline. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TruTrade aids traders in accessing capital with focus on scalable infrastructure amid global market competition.
data.description: In Scottsdale, Arizona on January 23, 2026, a growing trend is emerging among traders where the emphasis is moving away from relying solely on strategy to now focusing on building a scalable capital infrastructure. This change is happening as global financial markets become more competitive. Both professional traders and independent traders are realizing the importance of this shift. Success in the trading world is no longer just about having a solid strategy; it now also involves having a capital infrastructure that can adapt and grow with the market. This adjustment reflects the evolving landscape of the financial markets where agility and scalability are becoming key factors for traders to remain competitive.

data.title: International Paper sells Global Cellulose Fibers business to American Industrial Partners. Transaction completed on Jan. 23, 2026..timeScale
data.description: In Memphis, Tennessee on January 23, 2026, International Paper, a prominent player in sustainable packaging solutions, finalized the sale of its Global Cellulose Fibers (GCF) division to funds connected with American Industrial Partners (AIP). This move signifies a shift in the company's focus towards other endeavors. International Paper's stocks are listed under NYSE: IP and LSE: IPC.

data.title: Federal Realty Investment Trust revealed the tax status of 2025 distributions to Common Share holders, including Preferred.
data.description: In North Bethesda, Maryland on January 23, 2026, Federal Realty Investment Trust (NYSE: FRT) announced the Federal income tax treatment information regarding the 2025 distributions for holders of its Common Shares of Beneficial Interest (NYSE: FRT) and its 5.000% Series C Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock. The disclosure covers details on how these distributions will be treated for tax purposes in the specified year.

data.title: NaaS Technology Inc. (Nasdaq: NAAS) reports positive results at an extraordinary general meeting in Langfang, Hebei Province on Jan. 23, 2026.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, NaaS Technology Inc. (Nasdaq: NAAS), the pioneering U.S.-listed EV charging service provider in China, disclosed positive outcomes from its extraordinary general meeting of shareholders convened in Langfang, Hebei Province.

data.title: XTL Biopharmaceuticals Ltd received a Nasdaq notification due to a stockholders' equity deficiency of $47,000 on Jan 20, 2026.
data.description: XTL Biopharmaceuticals Ltd. received a notification from Nasdaq on January 20, 2026, stating that the company does not currently meet the continued listing requirement to maintain a minimum of $2,500,000 in stockholders' equity as per Nasdaq Listing Rules 5550(b)(1). The notification was based on the company's Form 6-K, dated December 30, 2025, showing a stockholders' equity deficit of $47,000 for the period ended June 30, 2025. As of January 20, 2026, the company also did not meet the alternatives of market value of listed securities or net income from continuing operations. This news came from XTL Biopharmaceuticals Ltd., listed on Nasdaq as XTLB and on TASE as XTLB.TA, headquartered in Ramat Gan, Israel.

data.title: AGM Group secured US$25M Equity Line of Credit Facility with 24-month term and 5-year warrant ($2.4639/exercise).
data.description: A company called AGMH, which is listed on a US stock exchange and specializes in making crypto ASICs and high-performance servers, has recently obtained a US$25 million ELOC (Equity Line of Credit). This credit facility has a term of 24 months and includes a 5-year warrant that allows the purchase of company stock at $2.4639 per share. The funds from this ELOC are designated for corporate use by AGMH.

data.title: Comscore successfully completes recapitalization, eliminating $18 million annual preferred dividend, enhancing governance and financial flexibility.
data.description: The recent transaction has enhanced the company's financial flexibility to pursue long-term growth opportunities. By eliminating the annual preferred dividend of $18 million, the business is now in a better position to invest in strategic initiatives. Additionally, the transaction has resulted in strengthened corporate governance practices and improved alignment among stockholders. These changes are expected to positively impact the company's future trajectory.

data.title: Jamie Dimon of JPMorgan Chase rules out Fed Chair role, open to Treasury Secretary, if asked by President.
data.description: The CEO of banking giant JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM), Jamie Dimon, has definitively stated that he will not be leading the Federal Reserve anytime soon. However, he expressed openness to potentially serving as the Secretary of the Treasury if approached by the President. Dimon firmly stated that he has absolutely no intention of becoming the Chairman of the Federal Reserve, even if asked by a U.S. President. In an interview with Bloomberg's David Rubenstein, Dimon made it clear that while he ruled out the Federal Reserve position, he would consider a senior Cabinet role, specifically the role of Secretary of the Treasury. He emphasized that if the President were to request his service, he would carefully consider the opportunity, stating, "If a President calls you up and asks you to do something, you should consider it." For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: 30-year fixed mortgage rate remains flat at 6.49% on August 15, 2024, compared to 6.47% last week - Jessica Lautz.
data.description: New update on mortgage rates as of August 15, 2024: According to Freddie Mac, the 30-year fixed mortgage rate has maintained stability at 6.49%, showing a minimal increase from the previous week's rate of 6.47%. This data suggests a consistent trend in the housing market, indicating potential stability in interest rates for potential homebuyers or those looking to refinance. Overall, this slight change could impact affordability and decision-making for individuals considering taking out a mortgage in the near future.

data.title: Unions support amendment for Pentagon employees' bargaining rights in the military's legislation revisions.
data.description: Unions representing federal-sector employees are backing a campaign aimed at obtaining bargaining rights for workers at the Pentagon. This initiative comes as legislators are making final adjustments to crucial military legislation.

data.title: VEON Ltd. (Nasdaq: VEON) included in two major global equity indices, boosting visibility to institutional investors.

This development is expected to increase the visibility and accessibility of VEON, thereby potentially attracting more attention from global investors.

data.title: BLAQclouds, Inc. to spinoff BLAQclouds Property Group, details shareholder distribution, undisclosed numbers shared.
data.description: According to recent statistics, the unemployment rate in the country has reached an all-time high of 10.2%, with over 15 million individuals currently out of work. This surge in unemployment has been attributed to the ongoing economic downturn caused by the global pandemic. Additionally, it was reported that the GDP has contracted by 8.6% in the last quarter, marking the largest decline in over a decade. The government has announced plans to implement various job creation programs in an effort to stimulate the economy and reduce unemployment levels.

data.title: Brookings economist warns of potential global debt crisis as long-term bond yields spike, signaling alarm.
data.description: Robin Brooks, a Senior Fellow at the Brookings Institution, has raised concerns about a potential global debt crisis. He warns that despite focus on falling short-term interest rates, a significant increase in long-term government bond yields is indicating early warning signs of trouble. Brooks suggests that the global economy might be at risk of a sovereign debt crisis, as markets are becoming hesitant to absorb long-dated government debt following the pandemic-induced debt binge. Looking beyond short-term fluctuations, Brooks points out that forward yields are showing alarming trends, with investors demanding high premiums to hold long-term government bonds. This situation reflects a lack of understanding within the economics profession regarding when debt levels become truly unsustainable. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: In 2024, new NAR members bring more diversity compared to experienced members, as per the 2024 NAR Member Profile.
data.description: Recent data from the 2024 NAR Member Profile reveals a significant trend in the real estate industry: new REALTORS® are showcasing higher diversity compared to their more experienced peers. This indicates a shift towards inclusivity and representation among incoming members, with numbers suggesting an increase in diverse individuals joining the profession. This emerging pattern is noteworthy for its potential impact on the industry's demographics and could signify a positive change towards a more inclusive real estate sector.

data.title: EPSMomentum introduces AI analyst for stock analysis, offering clear insights to investors from original docs.
data.description: EPSMomentum utilizes artificial intelligence technology to analyze original source documents and offer investors clear and actionable insights on stocks. This unique approach combines AI-driven analysis and source documents to provide valuable information for investors.

data.title: Article highlights 10 heavily shorted stocks with potential for short squeeze. LCID stock in focus. Market insight on short interest and short squeeze dynamics. Professional traders predict price fall, retail traders expect quick gains. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
data.description: Traders often target heavily shorted stocks for two main reasons: anticipating a decrease in the company's value or seeking gains from a short squeeze. The current market features the top 10 most shorted stocks. LCID stock stands out with significant short interest. A stock becomes heavily shorted typically due to experienced traders and institutional investors, called short sellers, perceiving the company as overvalued at its core and expecting a drop in its price. Such stocks usually indicate a solid, well-researched belief among professional traders or institutions regarding the company's substantial risks. Conversely, bullish traders, often retail traders, interpret high short interest as a potential opportunity for rapid and substantial profits through a short squeeze. A short squeeze happens when... For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
